name: Aiea Loop Trail
tagline: Lush forest loop above Pearl Harbor with WWII crash site remnants — underrated and uncrowded.
parking: Free lot at Keaiwa Heiau State Park
bestTime: Early morning for cooler temps; avoid after heavy rain (very muddy)
insiderTips: Look for the WWII C-47 cargo plane wreck about midway through the loop — easy to miss if you're not watching for it. Trail is about 4.8 miles with gentle elevation. Dogs OK on leash. Combine with a visit to the Keaiwa Heiau (ancient Hawaiian healing temple) at the trailhead.
